skill.name: documentation
skill.description: Documentation generation workflow covering API docs, architecture docs, README files, code comments, and technical writing.
skill.summary: Documentation generation workflow covering API docs, architecture docs, README files, code comments,...

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 72 potential security issues including 61 instances of shell command execution, 11 references to weak cryptographic algorithms, and 2 instances of system reconnaissance patterns. These patterns are highly unusual for a documentation skill and require manual review. The high density of external command invocations and security-related patterns suggests this skill may contain functionality beyond simple documentation generation.

high_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm References
high_findings.description: The skill contains 11 references to weak cryptographic algorithms. This is unusual for a documentation skill and may indicate the skill implements cryptographic functionality rather than just documenting it. Possible legitimate use: educational content about crypto vulnerabilities. Possible malicious: actual weak crypto implementation.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern detected at multiple locations. Unusual for documentation skill but could be educational content about crypto security.

medium_findings.title: Ruby/Shell Backtick Command Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill contains 61 instances of Ruby/shell backtick execution. While a documentation tool might legitimately invoke documentation generators via shell, the volume is unusual and warrants verification that commands are hardcoded and safe.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern detected at 61 locations. Could be legitimate documentation tool invocations or concerning command execution. Requires manual review of actual commands.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Patterns
low_findings.description: The skill contains 2 references to system reconnaissance patterns. This is highly unusual for a documentation skill and may indicate the skill performs system enumeration rather than just documenting code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern found in documentation skill context. Could be explaining security testing legitimately or actually performing recon.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Execution via Backticks
dangerous_patterns.description: Ruby/shell backtick execution detected at 61 locations in SKILL.md

dangerous_patterns.title: Cryptographic Weakness References
dangerous_patterns.description: Weak cryptographic algorithm patterns detected at 9 locations

content.value_statement: This skill automates the creation of technical documentation including API docs, README files, and architecture documents. It helps developers maintain consistent, professional documentation without manual effort.
actual_capabilities: Generate API documentation from code comments and signatures
Create README files with installation, usage, and contribution sections
Write architecture decision records and system documentation
Add inline code comments and docstrings to source files
Produce markdown-formatted technical guides and tutorials
Maintain documentation consistency across multiple files
limitations: Requires well-commented source code for best API doc results
Does not execute code to verify runtime behavior
May require manual review for complex technical concepts

use_cases.title: API Documentation Generation
use_cases.description: Automatically generate comprehensive API documentation from source code, including endpoints, parameters, and response types.

use_cases.title: Project README Creation
use_cases.description: Create polished README files with getting-started guides, installation instructions, and contribution guidelines.
use_cases.target_user: Open source maintainers and project leads

use_cases.title: Code Comment Enhancement
use_cases.description: Add or improve inline code comments and docstrings to improve code readability and maintainability.
use_cases.target_user: Development teams wanting better code documentation

prompt_templates.title: Basic README Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a README.md file for this project. Include sections for: Project title, Description, Installation, Usage, Contributing, and License.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating a new project README from scratch

prompt_templates.title: API Documentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate API documentation for the following code. Include endpoint descriptions, parameter details, request/response examples, and error codes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Documenting an API from source code

prompt_templates.title: Architecture Documentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create architecture documentation including system overview, component diagram descriptions, data flow, and design decisions for this codebase.

prompt_templates.title: Code Commenting
prompt_templates.prompt: Add comprehensive inline comments and docstrings to the following code. Explain complex logic, document parameters and return values, and add header comments to files.

output_examples.input: Generate a README for a Python CLI tool that processes CSV files
output_examples.output:
# CSV Processor CLI

A command-line tool for processing and transforming CSV files.

## Installation

```bash
pip install csv-processor
```

## Usage

```bash
csv-processor input.csv --output output.csv --transform uppercase
```

## Features
- Read and write CSV files
- Apply transformations
- Filter rows by condition
- Export to multiple formats

## Create User Order

**Endpoint:** `POST /users/{id}/orders`

Creates a new order for a specific user.

### Parameters
- `id` (path, required): User's unique identifier
- `items` (body, required): Array of order items
- `shipping_address` (body, required): Delivery address

### Response
```json
{
  "order_id": "ord_123",
  "status": "pending",
  "total": 99.99
}
```

best_practices: Provide clear, specific instructions about the documentation format and structure desired
Include existing documentation or code context when asking for updates
Review generated documentation for technical accuracy before publishing
Use consistent formatting and style guidelines across documentation
anti_patterns: Asking for documentation without providing source code or context
Expecting the skill to understand runtime behavior without execution
Relying solely on generated docs without manual technical review
Generating documentation for code with no comments or structure

faq.question: Can this skill generate docs for any programming language?
faq.answer: Yes, it supports most popular languages including Python, JavaScript, Java, Go, and Ruby. The quality depends on code structure and existing comments.

faq.question: Does it require access to a running application?
faq.answer: No, it generates documentation from static source code. You do not need a running application.

faq.question: Can it update existing documentation?
faq.answer: Yes, you can provide existing documentation and ask the skill to update, enhance, or synchronize it with code changes.

faq.question: What formats can it output?
faq.answer: Primary output is Markdown, but it can also generate HTML, plain text, and format for specific documentation systems.

faq.question: How accurate is the generated API documentation?
faq.answer: Accuracy depends on code quality. Well-structured code with clear function signatures and comments produces accurate docs. Complex logic may require manual review.

faq.question: Can it document microservices architectures?
faq.answer: Yes, it can document service interactions, API contracts, and system architecture when provided with the relevant code and configuration files.
